constructor.identified_role: initializes the desktop-auth result object consumed by the post-login auth/gameSSO pipeline
key_observations: Builds a result object rooted at the caller-provided destination and zero-initializes the field later copied into the play gate at +0x128.
Calls 0x140299B50 to initialize the embedded string slots and default state carried by the result object.
Returns the initialized object with +0x128 explicitly cleared before any response-specific parsing or promotion.

field_initialization.notes: This field later feeds the global play-launch gate at 0x140E58B98.

desktop_auth_orchestrator.identified_role: desktop-auth/gameSSO orchestration routine that allocates the result object, runs the auth branch, and promotes its fields into shared globals
key_observations: Calls 0x140299A90 at 0x14015C86E and immediately stores the returned operation/result state word into 0x140E58A70.
Copies the returned token/session fields into the later JX/global cache block during the 0x14015C8BC..0x14015CA5C region.

Copies the result object's +0x128 field into 0x140E58B98 at 0x14015CA61..0x14015CA67, proving the global play gate is sourced from the auth result object rather than invented independently by the scheduler.

play_launch_gate.identified_role: deferred play-launch continuation flag carried through auth completion and consumed once cached JX access-token state is ready
supporting_observations: 0x140156A17 primes 0x140E58B98 = 1 immediately before the scheduler's shield/oauth/check_token path.
0x140299FB0 requires 0x140E58B98 == 1 and a non-empty JX access token at 0x140E58AA0 before tail-jumping to the Bearer play-request builder at 0x14029B1D0.
If the access token is not yet ready, 0x140299FB0 clears 0x140E58B98 instead of launching, showing the flag is a one-shot continuation gate rather than a permanent mode bit.
0x14029B050 stores esi back into 0x140E58B98 during the nearby state-5 response/cleanup handler family, indicating that play-stage results can preserve or overwrite the deferred-launch state.
play_launch_gate.notes: No explicit schema literal was recovered on this pass, so the name is behavior-derived rather than string-proven.

scheduler_gate.identified_role: consumes the play_launch_pending_flag once cached token state is sufficient

request_builder.identified_role: builds the Bearer-authenticated public game launch request
key_observations: Uses 'Authorization: Bearer <JX_ACCESS_TOKEN>' with the cached access token at 0x140E58AA0.
Composes the endpoint from the cached OAuth/root slot 0x140E58B68 plus 'public/v1/games/YCfdbvr2pM1zUYMxJRexZY/play'.
Writes 0x140E58A70 = 5 and clears 0x140E58B98 when submitting the request.

bearer_play_stage.ordering_conclusion: The Bearer public/v1/games/.../play stage is downstream of desktop-auth completion. It only becomes runnable after the auth result object's +0x128 continuation flag has been promoted into 0x140E58B98 and cached JX access-token state exists.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.desktop_auth_config_cache: This pass does not change the earlier mapping of the cached Basic-auth/root bundle; it adds the missing control-flow field that tells the scheduler when to spend the cached JX access token on the later Bearer play request.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.display_name_sink_and_play_gate: This pass upgrades the earlier scheduler-only play-gate mapping by proving that the gate originates in the desktop-auth result object at +0x128.

updated_conclusion: 0x140E58B98 is best classified as a play-launch continuation flag sourced from the desktop-auth result object at +0x128. The desktop-auth orchestrator promotes that field into the global gate, and the scheduler later consumes it to fire the Bearer-authenticated public/v1/games/.../play request only after JX access-token state is ready.
next_targets: Find the exact parser or response branch that writes a non-zero value into result_object +0x128 so the play_launch_pending_flag can be named from a concrete setter rather than behavior alone.
Decode the nearby state-5 response handler around 0x14029B050 to identify which returned values are written back into 0x140E58B98.
Continue naming the third 0x1401303A0 caller rooted at 0x14013098F so the shared display-name sink is tied to a concrete subsystem.
